steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice best defines 'high-sinuosity river.' A definition must state the essential meaning of the term — not how it forms, behaves, or an example of one.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — A high-sinuosity river is one that has many meanders, or U-shaped curves.: states the essential meaning of the term directly. ✓
  • Choice B — A high-sinuosity river has U-shaped curves called meanders that will gradually change shape and shift downstream.: describes what meanders do, not what a high-sinuosity river is. ✗
  • Choice C — High sinuosity is caused by the erosion and redepositing of sediment in a riverbank over time.: tells how the river forms, not its definition. ✗
  • Choice D — Over time, the many meanders in the high-sinuosity Tanana River will change shape and migrate downstream.: illustrates the term with an instance rather than defining it. ✗
That matches Choice A — A high-sinuosity river is one that has many meanders, or U-shaped curves..
